record.field: Lists all available Odoo website building blocks (snippets) with their key, name and title. Use this to discover which building blocks exist before getting their code.

Retrieves the rendered HTML of a specific Odoo website building block (snippet). Use this to get the actual HTML structure of a building block before modifying it. The snippet_key should be in the format "website.s_name" (e.g., "website.s_cover", "website.s_text_image").

Applies changes to the website page currently being edited. PREFERRED for editing existing content: use action="edit_content" with section_index. You can combine content_replacements (to change text/HTML inside elements) and class_operations (to add/remove CSS classes) in the same call. For changing background color presets: use class_operations with no selector (targets the section itself). Example: action="edit_content", section_index=2, class_operations=[{"remove": "o_cc1", "add": "o_cc3"}] For changing text: use content_replacements with a CSS selector. Example: action="edit_content", section_index=1, content_replacements=[{"selector": "h1", "new_inner_html": "New Bold Title"}] For adding new sections: use action="append" or "prepend" with html. For removing sections: use action="remove" with section_index. NEVER invent image URLs — only use /web/image/website.* paths from building block templates.

ai['result'] = record._ai_tool_apply_html_to_page(html=html if html else '', action=action, section_index=section_index if section_index else None, content_replacements=content_replacements if content_replacements else None, class_operations=class_operations if class_operations else None)
Returns the current content of the user_custom_rules.scss file. Use this to see what custom styles are already applied before adding new ones.

Appends SCSS rules to the website's user_custom_rules.scss file. After saving, the page is automatically saved and CSS assets are reloaded. Only use this tool when predefined Odoo CSS classes cannot achieve the desired styling. The CSS content should be valid SCSS. Use Odoo SCSS variables when available.
